summary:
Overall UK risk remains Elevated. The near-term picture continues to be shaped by a mix of external security shocks and domestic fragility: (1) the Russia–Ukraine war remains high-intensity with ongoing long-range strike activity, (2) energy and inflation sensitivity persists even as the Ofgem price cap has eased for the current quarter, and (3) the UK cyber baseline remains elevated due to urgent patching requirements for widely deployed perimeter products.

Natural-hazard signals in the evidence reviewed today remain comparatively contained, and there is no indication in the sources reviewed of an acute UK constitutional or governance crisis. However, the combination of elevated conflict risk, oil-price sensitivity, and an elevated cyber advisory posture supports maintaining an Elevated overall stance.

categories.name: Economic / Financial
categories.rationale: Brent has pulled back versus yesterday’s reading but remains elevated enough to keep UK cost-of-living and inflation expectations sensitive to external shocks, while CPI remains above target despite easing household energy costs under the new cap.
drivers: Trading Economics: Brent crude shown around 90.38 USD/bbl (updated April 18, 2026). https://tradingeconomics.com/commodity/brent-crude-oil
ONS: CPI inflation was 3.0% in the 12 months to February 2026 (unchanged from January). https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/inflationandpriceindices/bulletins/consumerpriceinflation/february2026

Ofgem: energy price cap for 1 Apr–30 Jun 2026 is £1,641/year for a typical dual-fuel household paying by Direct Debit (a decrease of 6.6% vs £1,758). https://www.ofgem.gov.uk/information-consumers/energy-advice-households/energy-price-cap-explained

categories.name: Military / Security
categories.rationale: European security risk remains elevated due to the continuing high-intensity Russia–Ukraine war and ongoing long-range strike activity, while the UK domestic terrorism threat level remains SUBSTANTIAL.
drivers: MI5: UK national terrorism threat level is SUBSTANTIAL (an attack is likely). https://www.mi5.gov.uk/threats-and-advice/terrorism-threat-levels

Reuters reporting continues to indicate sustained Russia strike activity impacting Ukraine (context unchanged from prior update). https://www.reuters.com/world/russian-missiles-hit-ukraines-kyiv-officials-report-injuries-fires-2026-04-16/

categories.name: Political / Governance
categories.rationale: No new evidence reviewed today indicates acute UK institutional instability; governance posture appears stable with crisis-management activity focused on monitoring external shocks and domestic impacts.
drivers: GOV.UK: COBR(M) readout on domestic economic impacts linked to the Middle East situation (23 Mar 2026). https://www.gov.uk/government/news/readout-cobrm-iran-economic-and-domestic-impacts-23-march-2026

categories.name: Natural Hazards
categories.rationale: No major UK hazard event was identified in the evidence reviewed today; however, because the national flood-forecast headline could not be reliably extracted in this run, the assessment remains Guarded rather than being stepped down.
drivers: GOV.UK flooding service: current flood warnings and national flood forecast are available, but the national headline statement could not be reliably extracted in this run. https://check-for-flooding.service.gov.uk

categories.name: Social Stability
categories.rationale: Near-term disruption risk remains concentrated in planned London Underground strike action later in April, which is expected to reduce services and disrupt commuting.
drivers: Time Out: London Underground strike dates April 21–22 and April 23–24 (12pm to 11.59am windows). https://www.timeout.com/london/news/london-tube-and-train-strikes-spring-2026-full-list-of-dates-and-lines-impacted-how-to-travel-everything-you-need-to-know-march-april-may-031026

categories.name: Critical Infrastructure / Cyber
categories.rationale: Cyber/CNI risk remains elevated due to urgent patching requirements for widely deployed edge appliances (Citrix NetScaler), consistent with continued exposure to exploitation of internet-facing services.
drivers: NCSC: urges UK organisations to take immediate action on Citrix NetScaler vulnerabilities CVE-2026-3055 and CVE-2026-4368. https://www.ncsc.gov.uk/news/vulnerabilities-affecting-citrix-netscaler-adc-gateway

indicators.name: Brent crude
indicators.note: Trading Economics shows Brent crude at ~90.38 USD/bbl (updated April 18, 2026). https://tradingeconomics.com/commodity/brent-crude-oil

indicators.name: UK CPI inflation (12 months to Feb 2026)
indicators.note: ONS: CPI rose by 3.0% in the 12 months to February 2026, unchanged from January. https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/inflationandpriceindices/bulletins/consumerpriceinflation/february2026

indicators.name: Energy price cap (typical dual-fuel, Direct Debit)
indicators.note: Ofgem: price cap for 1 Apr–30 Jun 2026 is £1,641/year (down 6.6% vs £1,758 for 1 Jan–31 Mar 2026). https://www.ofgem.gov.uk/information-consumers/energy-advice-households/energy-price-cap-explained

indicators.name: UK terrorism threat level
indicators.note: MI5: UK national terrorism threat level is SUBSTANTIAL (attack is likely). https://www.mi5.gov.uk/threats-and-advice/terrorism-threat-levels

changes_since_last_update: Brent crude indicator refreshed to ~90.38 USD/bbl (Trading Economics, updated April 18, 2026), down versus the prior dashboard reading.
Military / Security trend moved from worsening to unchanged as no new escalation signal was confirmed in sources reviewed beyond the already-established elevated baseline.
priority_watch_items: Energy spillovers: watch for renewed oil-price spikes feeding into UK fuel prices and broader inflation expectations.
European security: monitor for further large-scale Russia–Ukraine strike waves and any impacts on Black Sea / regional infrastructure relevant to energy and commodity flows.
Transport disruption: monitor confirmed London Underground strike impacts (21–24 April) and any wider industrial-action spillover.
Perimeter vulnerability exposure: confirm remediation/patching progress for Citrix NetScaler and watch for exploitation indicators.
Flooding/Weather: monitor EA flood updates and Met Office warnings for any rapid shift into a higher-impact event.
source_notes: This update prioritises UK official sources (ONS, Ofgem, MI5, NCSC, GOV.UK) and established reporting for conflict context; Trading Economics is used for a Brent snapshot. https://tradingeconomics.com/commodity/brent-crude-oil

The Environment Agency flood service page could not be reliably extracted for the national 5-day headline statement during this run; Natural Hazards is therefore held at Guarded with explicit uncertainty. https://check-for-flooding.service.gov.uk
